reviews.review_text: Found a used condom on the bed frame we were sleeping on, management never replied back to email following it up.

Had an amazing stay here. Chalets are old school, reminds me of camping as a kid. Very laid back helpful staff. Park is overall neat and tidy. Extremely good value and in an excellent location. Thanks for having us

A very outdated park. The camp kitchen is ugly. The ablution blocks stink and really need a proper clean. I thought the location would be the advantage, but the camping sites are right next to a busy road, and it's very noisy even at night. It was also quite pricey for the conditions.
Would not come back.
Very accommodating even though we forgot a few things. Good and friendly service.  thanks

Dog friendly caravan park situated right between Busselton and Dunsborough.  Playgrounds,bouncy pillow, and mini golf available.  Sparkling pool with bbq area close by.  BBQ's are spotless. Clean amenities toilets and laundrys, camp kitchen has all you need. . A selection of accommodation available with lush lawn sites for caravans. Beach 200mtres away.  Staff extremely pleasant and always willing to assist. Very impressed

Unfortunately would not recommend this place. It is extremely run down and the facilities are very poor staff also not the friendliest. The cabins need some MAJOR upgrades/repairs, they are in such poor condition. When we went to make our dinner every pot and dish we got out of the cupboard was absolutely filthy and greasy so they obviously don’t check them after the previous guests. There were ants all through the kitchen.
The “main” bedroom had the smallest bed I have ever seen! It was more of a king single than a double bed…so we didn’t get a good nights sleep in the week we stayed.
Everything was so old, rusted and mouldy.
They advertise a pool, recreation hall and mini golf! The pool is very small, the hall has absolutely nothing in it for the kids and the mini golf looks like it’s located in a ghost town that hasn’t been touched for years….it is definitely not something they should advertise! Even the “basketball” ring was broken. So the only thing the kids had to do was bounce on the bouncing pillow!
I’m wondering if they are closing the park or selling as it is in such poor condition and they don’t seem to care??
It really was such a let down! It is in a lovely location but unfortunately is like staying in a run down trailer park in America.
Stains on sheets but Greta place otherwise
A good value family venue with a range of accommodation options

45$ a night
What we didn’t like:
-the sites are very close to the street, there was heavy traffic into late night, and it started up early in the morning
-the drinking water quality is bad, it leaves an aftertaste (to be fair so did the public fountain, so maybe this is a wider issue)
-the kitchen was bad, everything is kind of dirty and covered in grease
-the bathroom cleanliness could improve
-it had this unkept feeling, the facilities were good, but not nicely maintained
-the bins were so dirty, you didn’t want to touch them
-they have an airfryer that’s never been cleaned
-the hall with indoor entertainment is closed and evidently out of commission for a long while now
-the flier says that „check out is at 10 am, unless otherwise arranged with reception in advance” but when we asked for some extra minutes they said no (we were waiting for the dryer to finish the cycle, so I ended up leaving the premises with the car while my friend stayed behind waiting for the laundry).
-the WiFi didn’t work

What we liked:
-bathroom quite newly renovated
-3 washing machines, one dryer (6$)
- the trees and grounds looked really nice
- they had some entertainment stuff: 2 playgrounds, pool, jumping pillow, mini golf
-designated area for a dog bath

Caravan park has a pleasant situation with lots of trees. Our powered site was unfortunately quite tiny and we could only just squeeze our tent and vehicle in.  The camp kitchen was absolutely woeful. It only offered a fridge, toaster, a microwave and a filthy air fryer. No kettle, no cutlery and nothing to cook with. The shower toilet facilities a few and far between so unless you are fully self-contained this would not be a top choice caravan park.

Best thing....mostly shady sites
Worst thing ....too many live in ..who think they own the place..    sites very tight  .hard to get into...
Need to work on their responses to customer emails!

65$ la nuit pour 2 c’est assez excessif au vu de la qualité du camping…
Les blocs sanitaires étaient moyens, ce n’était pas vraiment propre et ça aurait besoin d’un bon coup de neuf.
Une cuisine ? On ne peut pas appeler ça une cuisine. L’état des équipements est à revoir, la plaque à induction est sûrement plus vieille que ma grand mère.
Sincèrement passer votre chemin.

this is not bad. im doing this just to say that the toliets are kinda dirty and the pool is just SOO SMALL so small that all the kids were there and i had no space to swim. not bad for this camp park.  Most fun part was the jumping pillow and the mini golf and the basketball court. the lady in the reception was super friendly. by the way if ur an adult DONT go to the park near e3 u might break the swing. kitchen= terrible.

Stayed in one of the so-called "new" chalets, but it was incredibly dirty (those floors haven't seen a vacuum or mop in a long time), the toilet seat was wobbly, and the flyscreen door was broken - both off the rail and had a giant flapping hole in it. But worst of all was the broken bed which ruined my back for the rest of our holiday. When we complained, there was no apology, no offer of compensation, in fact it felt like we were the ones inconveniencing them. And all for a price that was very high (an airbnb would've been cheaper). I'm giving 2 stars, which is generous, but the extra star is for the fact that they did change the bed the same day we complained, and for the number of peppermint trees on site.
Management  at the 4 seasons are allways happy to help and a great affordable  place place for getaway 😀

We had 4 different accommodations booked for 7 nights they’d booked 1 for only 6 nights, which meant we had to have our friends book out a day early because they travelled with us!|Their website was wrong it stated $210 per night for pet friendly cottage, they charged us $330 per night, we received a refund for that only after I threatened to take it further!|The condition of all properties was well below acceptable!  Mould in bathrooms bedrooms and showers not clean!  Dirty under all furniture! |No security!  People noisy at night, parties continuing until very late!|Washing machines out of order!|The list is very long!!|Will not be returning or definitely not recommending!

We stayed in a the onsite Caravan. We were disappointed in the accomodation.
- The bunk beds have no railing and so high for our children so they couldn’t sleep in them
- No running hot water to clean dishes (used the kettle)
- the sinks plug was so old it didn’t even block the sink to fill up, had mould under it, looked like it’s never been replaced.
- the caravan air vents had black mould / leafs, and dust
- possums were so loud at night, by day 3 we realised the possums were sleeping on top of the caravan and could see the possums through the air vent during the day.
- on the first night the smoke alarm kept going off every half hour
- no wifi
- only 1 working washing machine over new years period
- no microwave
A very run down park with little services

Only gave it a star because I had to . Woeful. Guess they have you because of location . Bring everything if you want to cook because facilities are useless. Lucky I didn’t chop my fingers off with the blunt knife in the kitchen and I am serious about that. Both laundry’s contained out of order washing machines. The whole holiday Park was at massive capacity and from what we saw only two washing machines were available. When raising it with the staff we were told that Busselton apparently has laundrettes ? Isn’t it a facility that the holiday park incorporates in the rental fee? The only good thing about this place is the hard working people that are holidaying here and need a break from work and have had to pay the exorbitant fees and get ripped off. Very sad for them .

Terrible experience – zero customer service

This holiday park was extremely disappointing. The washing machines do not work, yet they still take your money. My coins were swallowed and I couldn’t do any laundry at all.

What made it even worse was the reaction from staff. Instead of apologising or helping, I had to argue for a long time just to get my money back. I was actually told it was my fault and that I was to blame. That is completely unacceptable.

There was no accountability, no friendliness, and absolutely no customer service. If basic facilities like washing machines don’t work and guests are then blamed for it, something is seriously wrong with the management.

I would not recommend this place to anyone. If you expect working facilities and respectful treatment, stay somewhere else.
A great relaxing park..amenities are spotless ,service fantastic and a great location

I must agree with the negative reviews. I used to like the place, but it’s in the past. I payed one more day than I could stay and asked for refund 2 hours later, but no grace. And we used to pay $30 or so for a family unpowered, now same family $105 powered. I was really disappointed about current so called policies.

The house that I stayed in was nothing like the photo is was a run down waterless place I think we just got unlucky with the house because others were better the vibes were good overall and the services were very helpful to fix our water
ITS A GREAT QUIET HOMLEY PLACE
45 dollars a night for unpowered! What a joke. Find somewhere else. Read all the other 1 star comments for an idea of this place. Kitchen is a joke.
Lovely old camping area.Good value of money.

This is our favourite (now only) place we book accommodation when heading down south. The chalets that are dog friendly are really good. Has everything you need to head away with our dogs. Bring your own bedding and all kitchen amenities supplied. Very easy check in and check out. Staff are always friendly and happy to assist. Ideally situated as basically half way between Busselton and Dunsborough.  Thank you for another enjoyable stay.

Love this caravan park.  Safe for a solo female traveller. Clean grassed and paved sites nestled amongst shady trees, bus stop out the front, lovely pool, ample play area's for kids,  including mini golf and jumping castle.  Very impressed

We were able to get an onsite van at very short notice before the long weekend , and while we didn’t have high expectations (due to the last min booking) we were really happy with our accomodation and our holiday. The staff were so polite and helpful, the accomodation was fairly priced and the kids had plenty to do. We’ll definitely stay again!

I’ve been coming to Four Seasons Holiday Park for over 10 years, but the standards have gone downhill badly. The couches and furniture haven’t been replaced in over a decade — they’re old, uncomfortable, and look like something people would throw out on the side of the road. Yet management keeps increasing prices, charging nearly double what it used to cost, without putting any of that money back into the accommodation.

On top of that, they hit tenants with an extra $500 “cleaning fee” whether the unit is left clean or not. Many long-term guests have been charged unfairly, which feels dishonest and exploitative. When Bill owned the place, things were fair and looked after — now it feels like profit is the only priority.

Very disappointed after so many years of loyalty. Poor value, outdated facilities, and unfair charges. I would not recommend staying here unless major changes are made.

I booked this carvan because it is a dog friendly one. However, I highly do not recommend this place. Despite the not so good ratings I saw on this park, i wanted to give it a try. However, it is very true, the place is verrryyy old, super run down, they are loads of permanent residents and i do agree to one of the other comments, being a young female i did not feel so comfortable in my tent.
The toilets didn’t flush properly and it was just disgusting. I rather would have peed outside somewhere. The toilet seats were moving and there was poop in all the toilets and the whole bathroom was flooded with water from the shower and attracted a lot of mossies.. Unfortunately, i came after hours and i was already given a tent spot, which turned out to be right behind the massive entry sign, where the cars were driving past all night. When I drove around tho, i saw heaps of empty camp spots, which clearly showed that the staff does not care. Not about the facilities and not about its campers.

For people, who are wanting to have a calm and relaxing holiday. This is definitely not the place. I want to save people form getting disappointed staying there and wasting their money.
Absolutely perfect campsite. Great location and has everything you need
A great place to stay , very reasonably priced with all the amenities on site, the staff are very helpful
Good place to stay, lots of places to visit near the area

Four Seasons has been an awesome place to stay. The staff (Park Management, Cleaners and Maintenance Crew) have been amazing to deal with. They are pet friendly and very accommodating to your needs. Highly recommend this park. Thank you.

Just got home from a 3 night 4 day trip. What a great spot. Clean amenities,  friendly staff and good service.  Had one little issue with the onsite van that was promptly rectified  when staff were informed.  For those that whinge about the traffic, maybe don't pick a park on the main road. We personally found it a peaceful place to spend some time. Can't wait to get back down for a few nights with the camper. Spot already paid for. Thanks 4 seasons
great experience, very friendly staff. we stayed one night, checked in & out after hours without issues. thank you!

Very run down place . Need to separate visitors from permanent resident. Feel un safe at night.
Room was clean but …..bed was really uncomfortable need mattress upgrade . No privacy with no doors to separate bathroom. Shower curtains used instead. Not hygienic . Toilet dripped all night. Stove had sign advised us before. electric fry pan not useable for anything as scratched everything sticks to it.
no smoke detectors. Massive safety risk.  Could not wait to get out

Perfect hub to explore the surrounding areas, the ablution block and bbqs have recently been updated to a good standard. There are a lot of residents on site, most are ok, some act as though they own the place and that park rules do not apply. I had seen, on multiple occasions, dogs not only off lead, but with no collar.
Inaddition, there are many, what look like abandoned trailers, caravans, portable buildings and old building materials scattered around the edge of the camp.
Toilet block cleaning was at 7am....
And staff get around in noisy petrol powered golf carts.

Came down n stayed 4 nights with children n partners and 5 young granddaughters. Everything perfect , pool and bouncy balloon were a definite hit with the girls. Felt very safe letting them scoot around on scooters n bikes. Would highly recommend for a relaxing break.

This place looks like a Soweto slum. So many permanent shacks with a sprinkling of visitor sites amongst them. Very sparse grass, low water pressure, washing machines out of order, dirty bathrooms despite claims they are cleaned twice daily. Extremely tight sites, streets and trees to negotiate. I could almost reach out the window and touch the camper tent beside our van - no privacy whatsoever and very limited parking space. Only nice part was clean bbqs and the office staff. Couldn't wait to leave. Really need to have a dedicated "visitor" section with larger easily accessible sites and keep the permanent section separate. For $45 a night with no wifi, they need to lift their game.

The most uncleaned accomodation we have ever stayed at! Booked a non pet friendly cabin we opened the door and instantly smelt like dogs. Couch was full of dog hair, film of dirt on all surfaces and walls, floors were nor clean either. The most uncomfortable weve ever been we will never book here again.

Honestly I think some people are just whingers! Easy to manoeuvre large vehicles. Lovely lawns & beautiful trees. Clean facilities. Pet friendly. Close walk to amazing beach.
Yes there are a lot of permanent residents, didn't have an issue with them.
It's an awesome caravan park, I'm unsure about rooms though. It's set up really well with the beach close by and  there's a lot of places to see around this area also.

Une nuit. Presque 200$, pour dormir dans un demi mobile-home avec un lit bloqué dans des trous au sol.
Un sol sale , plein de poussière et de miettes. Les chaussettes blanches ont fini noires en 10 min ....
On devait recevoir un questionnaire de satisfaction on ne l'a bien-sûr jamais eu...

staff were lovely
nice clean amenities
good location
a nice beach over the road

shaded unpowered sites 👍

lots of activities inside the park, would have been nice to have had access to the pool table in evening though

trailer park boys vibes -love it
A wonderful caravan park well shaded and well kept all service's. A great spot for visiting the areas.

Hard one to review.

Pro’s -
- Amazing reception staff (Dianne, what a pleasure)!
- The fresh water and powered sites with a concrete slab are well cared for and have a wonderful canopy of peppermint trees which helps keep things cool in summer (albeit messy when the wind hits).
- Possums everywhere which are fun to spot at night.
- Availability at short notice was great
- The ground staff and cleaners work hard on keeping things tidy and managed
- facilities with the pool, dog wash area, gaming hall, and the Pizza night, was great.
- Laundry and ablutions were handy. Cleaners do a great job, morning and night, to keep them in check. Friendly staff too.
- DOG FRIENDLY, which suited us. Leads at all times.
- Essentials available to purchase, at reception, is handy when you’ve forgotten something at the supermarket

Con’s -
- There were some intimidating full time residents in the park which made for some discomfort
- As much as we loved and needed the shade of the peppermint trees, boy are they messy if your set up is predominantly outdoor. The blowers from grounds keepers do start early so no sleep ins :)
- Don’t pick a site near the pool and basketball area if you value rest and peace… it’s an all day and all night noise fest
- It’s not beach front, however, it’s only a 200m walk across a somewhat busy road, to get to the water.
- The ablution blocks are very handy with warm fresh showers and plenty of them between a couple of blocks however they do get busy and not everyone cleans up after themselves (always the way)
- Laundry is great ($6 for washing machine, $5 for dryer I think and BYO detergent) but a few of the dryers aren’t working so you may have to wait your turn or bring pegs for the clothes lines

All in all it was a great place to stay at a very reasonable price. The staff are the highlight and the facilities are really useful if you aren’t fully self sufficient. It’s a good spot for those travelling with dogs as the beach close by is also dog friendly.

If you are looking for a quite and relaxing camping, don’t choose this holiday park.

The campsite is too close to the main road where there are continuously trucks and cars traffic, day and night. Lucky we booked for 2 nights only because and I couldn’t sleep at all!!

Overall the camping is very nice and clean with good staff. The beach is also beautiful and just cross the road.
Workers have no respect 👎

DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY
Place is so run down and they should not be renting some cabins due to age and safety.
- unclean cabin - glasses dirty, toilet
- no flyscreens and a broken window in the kids bedroom (safety hazard reported during stay)
- pots and pans all peeling unable to use, had to see reception - wouldnt cook for my dog in them
- dirty couch
- dirty aircon filter with all bugs everywhere when you turn it on
-windows on bedrooms dont open as so old
- no lamp shade, art all wonky, like its a left over room noone has checked
- no basics like dishwashing liquid or broom to tidy up, had to see reception again
- play equipment full of cob webbs and noone around in sight, i had to clean so kids would use
- jumping pillow is located next to a shed with some rough undesirables living in there. I felt uneasy.

Housekeeping had inappropriate music with swearing cranked during the day and it wss really tacky and felt like we were on someones block not at a park.

Our cabin was so run down we spoke to reception during the stay and they appreciated our comments at the time and said the park has not been compliant by the council however understood our complaint but was fully booked.

We followed up with an email to the manager to let her know how dissapointed we were with the cabin and facilities and she gave us a tiny discount off our next stay, we requested a partial refund as we mentioned these issues during the stay and the manager has ignored that, in our eyes its not about the cabin its about how management respond to your concerns (which are valid)

We were celebrating 2 x birthdays and would highly recommend avoiding this park and paying full price somewhere else where you will be looked after and there is an atmosphere.

Reception girl was lovely and location was ok, however as above, do not recommend.

Four Seasons Trailer Park*

Pros:
- Friendly staff who are willing to help out. Red Frogs security team were fabulous, very informative and friendly. Maintenance crew were quickly responsive to our power outage.
- The food provided was adequate and filling.

Cons:
- The rooms were dirty, there was several leaks in the roof, and mold inside the cabin from the leaks. Utensils were dirty and needed a pre wash before use, the water in our cabin wasn't safe to drink and cooking appliances were faulty, causing our group to venture out to another cabin in order to get water and cook.
- Security faults, the back window was unable to lock, and could easily be slid open from the outside.

This place is always our first option when coming south as its dog friendly 299m walk to the beach, pool and we have a fair idea of the rules, our last trip weekend 28/11/24 was a last minute booking and we were lucky to get a spot we were down row E the toilet blocks were cleaned in the morning before our showers which was great because the bugs this weekend was crazy and everywhere, place has changed a little many places no longer have gates and fence barriers but the dog still enjoyed his stay, we will keep coming for as long as our dog will travel with us.
Just a suggestion would It be possible to have the e scooters that we pay for online for short rides up and down caves road parked close to the park on weekends and long weekend as that would have been perfect I know that’s not under your control but if there was a change to have something like that organized would be great
Terrible place. Never stay here.
Best holiday park in the area, really clean bathrooms and kitchen, and all around high quality for what you pay

Camping plutôt bien situé pour la région et proche de la plage. En revanche il n'est plus vraiment neuf, certains équipements sont sales et les sanitaires femmes n'étaient pas vraiment propres. Pour le prix de la région cela passe, mais ce n'est pas le grand luxe.

When booking a caravan site, for the long weekend, I was told there were sites available, however on arrival, the site we were given was right down the back of the park directly in front of the gate of a newly constructed workshop shed which was meant to be given unresticted access. When the park is full we were told this site can be used. We were wedged in between a large bus on one side and a metal fence on the other overlooking a permanent resident's van. We asked if we could move to another site but the only available one left , as the park was fully booked,  was close to the road and not suitable for our 22ft van. We appreciated the helpful assistance given to us at that stage by your groundsman.
We would have appreciated being told about the site prior to making the booking. Another more serious issue - the double powerpoint mounted on the fence was not of an external weatherproof design and poses a safety issue when wet. This caravan park is in need of significant upgrades and improvements.
Homes people live there we where ment to stay 3 weeks but we ended up at 2 weeks  we got put in the people who lives there and they were OK but the rules were really bad

I wasn't going to bother reviewing but the receptionist was so rude when I mentioned how filthy the showers are when I checked out I just have to. She wasn't particularly nice when I checked in, quite derogatory about a fellow who obviously lives in the park who she was serving when I walked in as well.
But gee wizz. Telling me the showers are actually clean and asking me when I walked in (afternoon, they still smelled very bleach), making me out to be a liar was really not necessary... the kind of filth on the grout and the mould takes time to get that bad and isn't created just by whoever used the facilities before me.
It's a real shame. It was a nice old-style park. The amenities have those new oversized tiles but are old and the cubicles tiny. The camping area is nice, out under trees though miles from their camper's  kitchen (located surrounded by caravans / powered sites, I only saw campers using the kitchen lol).

It should have been a great park. Funny how one person can ruin everything. I will not go back, and that's a massive disappointment to me. Zero stars.

Booked here for 3 nights camping, it's so good we stayed for 6.
Amenities are clean and nice!
Staff are friendly and helpful
Dog beach right there!
We found a gem here!

This carravan park is clean and private.
Good security and on site vans.
We

Affordable for a family and great facilities for the kids and our four legged friend. Dogs are welcome and they even have a dog wash facility. It’s not the usual concrete caravan park full of identical cabins painted grey. This park actually has character and trees and lots of grass. And a great playground . How caravan parks used to be. We will be  visiting again very soon. The owners of this park should be proud as it’s a real nice place to be. Thank you

Perfect spot, nice roomy Cottages and On Site Caravans, clean toilet blocks, plenty for kids to do, though a little bit confusing where we were supposed to park for us first timers, but all in all we loved our time there.
The park was well laid out and very accommodating for both dogs and owners. The amenities were clean and well maintained. Thank you for a lovely break
Stayed with 2 dogs 🐕.....just loved it ❤️ desk staff excellent

Great camp but the unpowered sites are at the back of the campsite and far from the facilities.
There is a big road just next to the camp, so it's not really quiet. Facilities are pretty clean. No WiFi but 4G reception good.
Los baños muy sucios. El sector sin electricidad con buena sombra, pero sin césped ni luz cerca.

This is a very Peaceful Patch. We stayed for 4 weeks in November 2023 in a very clean and comfortable Cabin, surrounded by well maintained lawns, trees and swimming pools. Excellent facility for our two puppies. Front of house office staff are very knowledgeable and helpful.|Once again, a thousand thanks for the Marybrook Four Seasons Holiday Park.
Perfect right next to the dog beach. Relaxing.
Atmosphere here was great, especially New Years Eve. However, the amenities and staff were of a low quality
Clean, Staff very helpful.
Very nice cabins, great holiday park. Beach right across the road
Stayed for two nights in a campervan. The powered sites are in between all of the permanent spots so not much room. Amenities were mostly clean but old. Price was fine but the Wifi was terrible.

Hired a 6 birth van for 3 nights,
The inside tables, floors, chairs had a film of dirt. The beds had no linen other than fitted sheet. They dropped 3 doona's and 3 pillows on the outside table while I was out.  Pity they didn't take them in to the beds they might of seen  the top bunk beds and queen bed had a film of dead midgees/mossies all over them.. The glasses were dirty and the pots telflon coatings where severely scratched nicely flaking....unusable.
The outside table and chairs were totally covered in soot... Looking forward to a sleep-in
Woken at 6.30-45am to the sweet sound of a battery powered blower squeeling away at the toilet block next to the van. Went to use the toilet and shower at 7am it was being cleaned and you needed hazmat suit to negotiate the toxic strength bleach soaked floor and a helmet as the caution slippery floor sign wouldnt save you rushing for a morning pass. Why would you clean toilets at peak hour? My boys went to the rec room for a game of pool not pool cue in site..pretty disgracefully presented room as is the cleanliness of the site for the location.
On a good note lady at front desk was lovely and its pretty cheap.

The staff were friendly and welcoming.
The showers were hot hot hot.
25 dollaridoos a night for a non powered.
It's nothing fancy but hey neither am I

From appearances the front section would have been better stay. We stayed in an on-site caravan with added annex. Won't be staying in an on-site caravan again. It was roomy but maintenance standards are not up to date and non drinkable tap water.

Not a well managed park .Toilets were broken when we arrived and were still broken after eight days, locks on doors were broken  shower head was also broken .Toilets were supposed to be cleaned early in the morning but were still dirty. People had dogs running loose around the park without leads on. My partner was attacked twice by different dogs and I was bailed up by 2 German Shepherds near the  toilet block . Parents had no control over their children they were running wild all hours of the night.We made a complaint to the woman in the office and she acted as she didn't want to know about it.
Will never stay there again  and will not recommend it. Something has to be done about the problems and management.
Stayed 3 nights, 4th was free.  Friendly park staff and an enjoyable stay 😁 And dog friendly cabins available

Its certainly what you pay for. The beds are comfortable enough, but the lack of proper ladders on the bunks is rather reckless. The on-site caravan my friends and I stayed in only had running bore water, meaning any water we needed had to be sourced from the camp kitchen, a good 100 metre walk away, which isn't very handy come dinner time. The caravan itself was freezing, especially at night. But the park itself was nice. My friends and I, even if we are "adults" had a blast with the giant pillow and the park being right between Busselton and Dunsborough was very handy. Overall, it was okay.
